chunk_summary: This section outlines the ten-step care model for managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM), focusing on stabilization and rehabilitation phases, as recommended by WHO and national guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the ten-step care model for managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM).
Identify key interventions during stabilization and rehabilitation phases of SAM management.
Learn evidence-based guidelines for facility-based care of children with SAM.
Explore the role of nutritional and medical protocols in reducing mortality and promoting recovery.
key_facts: The ten-step care model includes stabilization (acute phase) and rehabilitation (recovery phase).
WHO guidelines emphasize treating hypoglycemia, hypothermia, and dehydration during stabilization.
Nutritional oedema and wasting are critical conditions addressed in SAM management protocols.
Facility-based management guidelines by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (India) were established in 2011.
SAM management requires careful monitoring of electrolyte imbalances and micronutrient supplementation.
reference_quote: WHO guidelines recommend treating hypoglycemia, hypothermia, and dehydration as critical steps during the stabilization phase of SAM management. (Source: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK154454/)
document_key_topics: Ten-step care model for SAM management
